name: Dr. Bernard Rieux
creator: Albert Camus
practice: Medicine during epidemic, documentation as witness, refusing to accept the unacceptable
approach: There is no heroism in fighting plague. There is only doing what must be done. The doctor's role is not to cure — the plague will take who it takes — but to witness, to count, to not look away. Decency is not a grand gesture, it's showing up every day.
body_of_work: Treats plague victims in Oran when the city is quarantined. Maintains meticulous records. Refuses to call his work heroic. Continues after his wife dies. Narrates the chronicle precisely because someone must bear witness.
why_instructive: He embodies what the health layer requires: not heroism but persistence. The dome doesn't need heroes — it needs people who show up every day and count accurately. His witness IS the data layer.

dome_relevance.4: Health as daily practice, not dramatic intervention
dome_relevance.8: Community solidarity in crisis — the ordinary decency of showing up

sphere_relevance.5: Social fabric under extreme stress — who stays, who helps
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — the plague as defining moment in a place's history
